findings.title: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.Elem
findings.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

tool_specific.issue_text: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

findings.title: File contains sensitive information written directly, such as usernames, passwords, keys, etc
findings.description: File contains sensitive information written directly, such as usernames, passwords, keys, etc.
findings.recommendation: Credentials must not be stored in the Git code or repository. There are ‘Secrets Management’ solutions that can be used to store secrets or use Pipeline resources.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_admin" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant current_user.is_admin() because current_user.is_admin is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.correctness.useless-assignment
findings.description: `document.cookie` is assigned twice; the first assignment is useless
findings.code_excerpt:
document.cookie = `username=${encodeURIComponent(username)}; expires=${expires.toUTCString()}`;
    document.cookie = `hashedPassword=${encodeURIComponent(hashedPassword)}; expires=${expires.toUTCString()}; secure; HttpOnly`;

tool_specific.lines:
document.cookie = `username=${encodeURIComponent(username)}; expires=${expires.toUTCString()}`;
    document.cookie = `hashedPassword=${encodeURIComponent(hashedPassword)}; expires=${expires.toUTCString()}; secure; HttpOnly`;

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'run' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'run' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true
findings.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.insecure-hash-algorithm-md5
findings.description: Detected MD5 hash algorithm which is considered insecure. MD5 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.avoid_hardcoded_config_SECRET_KEY
findings.description: Hardcoded variable `SECRET_KEY` detected. Use environment variables or config files instead
findings.code_excerpt: app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key'  # Replace with your secret key

tool_specific.lines: app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key'  # Replace with your secret key

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).
findings.code_excerpt: const secret = 'your_secret_key'; // replace with your application's secret key

tool_specific.lines: const secret = 'your_secret_key'; // replace with your application's secret key

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-jwt-not-revoked
findings.description: No token revoking configured for `express-jwt`. A leaked token could still be used and unable to be revoked. Consider using function as the `isRevoked` option.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.hardcoded-jwt-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.pycryptodome.security.insufficient-rsa-key-size
findings.description: Detected an insufficient key size for RSA. NIST recommends a key size of 3072 or higher.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.jwt.security.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_admin" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant self.is_admin() because self.is_admin is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_premium" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant self.is_premium() because self.is_premium is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.correctness.return-in-init
findings.description: `return` should never appear inside a class __init__ function. This will cause a runtime error.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_admin" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant self.user.is_admin() because self.user.is_admin is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_json" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant request.is_json() because request.is_json is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_authenticated" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant request.user.is_authenticated() because request.user.is_authenticated is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.boto3.security.hardcoded-token
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.generic.secrets.gitleaks.hashicorp-tf-password
findings.description: A gitleaks hashicorp-tf-password was detected which attempts to identify hard-coded credentials. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.formatted-sql-query
findings.description: Detected possible formatted SQL query. Use parameterized queries instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.sqli.psycopg-sqli
findings.description: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a psycopg2 Python SQL statement. This could lead to SQL injection if the variable is user-controlled and not properly sanitized. In order to prevent SQL injection, use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead. You can use prepared statements by creating a 'sql.SQL' string. You can also use the pyformat binding style to create parameterized queries. For example: 'cur.execute(SELECT * FROM table WHERE name=%s, user_input)'
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.sqlalchemy.security.sqlalchemy-execute-raw-query
findings.description: Avoiding SQL string concatenation: untrusted input concatenated with raw SQL query can result in SQL Injection. In order to execute raw query safely, prepared statement should be used. SQLAlchemy provides TextualSQL to easily used prepared statement with named parameters. For complex SQL composition, use SQL Expression Language or Schema Definition Language. In most cases, SQLAlchemy ORM will be a better option.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.injection.tainted-sql-string
findings.description: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using an object-relational mapper (ORM) such as Sequelize which will protect your queries.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
findings.code_excerpt: db.query(`SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%${name}%'`, (err, results) => {

$SQLSTR.abstract_content: SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%

tool_specific.lines: db.query(`SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%${name}%'`, (err, results) => {

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.requests.best-practice.use-timeout
findings.description: Detected a 'requests' call without a timeout set. By default, 'requests' calls wait until the connection is closed. This means a 'requests' call without a timeout will hang the program if a response is never received. Consider setting a timeout for all 'requests'.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.unsafe-formatstring
findings.description: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a util.format / console.log function. If an attacker injects a format specifier in the string, it will forge the log message. Try to use constant values for the format string.
cwe_ids: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.html-in-template-string
findings.description: This template literal looks like HTML and has interpolated variables. These variables are not HTML-encoded by default. If the variables contain HTML tags, these may be interpreted by the browser, resulting in cross-site scripting (XSS).
cwe_ids: CWE-116: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.browser.security.insecure-document-method
findings.description: User controlled data in methods like `innerHTML`, `outerHTML` or `document.write` is an anti-pattern that can lead to XSS vulnerabilities
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.browser.security.insecure-innerhtml
findings.description: User controlled data in a `commentDiv.innerHTML` is an anti-pattern that can lead to XSS vulnerabilities
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-fs-filename
findings.description: Detected that function argument `req` has entered the fs module. An attacker could potentially control the location of this file, to include going backwards in the directory with '../'. To address this, ensure that user-controlled variables in file paths are validated.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: trailofbits.python.tarfile-extractall-traversal.tarfile-extractall-traversal
findings.description: Possible path traversal through `tarfile.open($PATH).extractall()` if the source tar is controlled by an attacker
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
findings.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.hardcoded-config.avoid_hardcoded_config_SECRET_KEY
findings.description: Hardcoded variable `SECRET_KEY` detected. Use environment variables or config files instead
findings.code_excerpt: app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key'  # Replace with your secret key

tool_specific.lines: app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key'  # Replace with your secret key

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: javascript.express.security.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret.express-jwt-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).
findings.code_excerpt: const secret = 'your_secret_key'; // replace with your application's secret key

tool_specific.lines: const secret = 'your_secret_key'; // replace with your application's secret key

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-jwt-not-revoked.express-jwt-not-revoked
findings.description: No token revoking configured for `express-jwt`. A leaked token could still be used and unable to be revoked. Consider using function as the `isRevoked` option.

findings.title: javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.jwt-hardcode.hardcoded-jwt-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: python.pycryptodome.security.insufficient-rsa-key-size.insufficient-rsa-key-size
findings.description: Detected an insufficient key size for RSA. NIST recommends a key size of 3072 or higher.

findings.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.flask-url-for-external-true.flask-url-for-external-true
findings.description: Function `flask.url_for` with `_external=True` argument will generate URLs using the `Host` header of the HTTP request, which may lead to security risks such as Host header injection
cwe_ids: CWE-673: External Influence of Sphere Definition
